title: Bitfarms Reports Fiscal Year 2025 Results
text: Bitfarms reported fiscal year 2025 results showing substantial strategic progress amid transitional challenges. Full-year 2025 revenue reached $229 million, representing 72% increase from prior year's $192.88 million. The company achieved significant capacity expansion with energized MW growing from 461 MW to over 700 MW through Stronghold Digital acquisition integration and infrastructure development. The strategic pivot to HPC/AI infrastructure accelerated during 2025 with initial deployment at Panther Creek, Pennsylvania site. Management announced plans to complete Bitcoin mining operations wind-down during 2026-2027 period, focusing full operational capacity on high-performance computing and AI applications. The company maintained 1,166 BTC in treasury at year-end, providing liquidity cushion for operations and capital projects. Capital expenditures remained disciplined at approximately $100 million during 2025 following Paraguay facility divestiture proceeds deployment. Management emphasized company's differentiation through substantial power portfolio, strategic U.S. geographic footprint, and infrastructure development capabilities. The transformation to Keel Infrastructure completed on April 1, 2026, reflecting strategic realignment toward energy and compute infrastructure focus. Analyst sentiment reflected cautious optimism regarding HPC/AI opportunity sizing and execution capabilities.
